items.title: Do Your Doritos Taste Different? #food #shorts
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>Some of your favorite snacks are being made differently since the government banned Red Dye no. 3 and food activists are pushing for more natural ingredients. PepsiCo designed a new spicy potato chip without using its signature—and artificially formulated—dyes. But remaking the original Doritos is proving to be much harder.

https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2025-03-03/snack-makers-are-removing-fake-colors-from-processed-foods</article>

items.title: How to Invest: Advice from top experts
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content: <article>#BloombergInvest guests tell us their take on the markets, from the best investment advice to money myths.</article>

items.title: How the League One Volleyball Came Together | The Deal

items.content:
<article>League One Volleyball Co-Founder Katlyn Gao shares with Alex Rodriguez how the vision for the league came together.
Watch the full episode of The Deal on the Bloomberg app or listen wherever you get your podcasts https://trib.al/lb8tnhv</article>

items.content:
<article>Jonathan Gray, President & Chief Operating Officer, Blackstone talks about the deals landscape in 2025 and where the firm is putting capital to work around AI with Bloomberg's Sonali Basak at Bloomberg Invest.

items.title: Sports as an Asset Class
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>It’s no secret that sports are growing as a major asset class, attracting those who have a passion for the industry and who also want to garner a meaningful profit… but how did this transition happen? We speak with IMG alum George Pyne and Sixth Street’s Josh Empson about the history of investing in sports and some investment trends today. We also interview Nicole Pullen-Ross of Goldman Sachs about investors’ desirability to add sports-related ventures to their portfolios.
--------

items.title: Why are fewer Black students attending Harvard Law School?
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>A year after the US Supreme Court banned race-based admissions, Harvard Law School's first-year Black student enrollment has plummeted — with repercussions beyond the classroom.

Janet Lorin explains https://trib.al/fguot1f</article>

items.title: China’s Economic Challenges With Lawrence H. Summers
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>Former US Treasury Secretary and Wall Street Week contributor Lawrence H. Summers breaks down China’s economic future as it faces challenges including consumer confidence, tariffs imposed by President Donald Trump, a weak housing market and foreign investment outflows. Elizabeth Economy from the Hoover Institute also tells us what priorities China will focus on at its National People’s Congress meeting that began this week.
--------

items.title: Estee Lauder: What's Next for the Company That Owns Jo Malone, Bobbi Brown, MAC?
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>Estée Lauder CEO Stéphane de La Faverie says he is committed to reigniting growth and achieving "solid double-digit margin" for the company. "We need to be reactive to where the consumer goes, and we need to make sure we give them the innovation they are looking for in the time that they want it," he said. 

The beauty and cosmetic giant recently announced its "Beauty Reimagined" plan to try and improve profitability and lift the company's struggling share price. De La Faverie joins "The Pulse with Francine Lacqua" exclusively on Bloomberg TV on March 7.
--------
More on Bloomberg Television and Markets

items.title: EU could replace Elon Musk's Starlink in skies Over #Ukraine #tech #shorts
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content: <article>French satellite operator Eutelsat Communications SA said talks with the European Union to replace Elon Musk’s Starlink in Ukraine are intensifying as the US pulls back from its commitments there.</article>

items.title: Ukraine, Russia: Europe Must Increase Defense Spending Rapidly, Kubilius Says
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>European Defense Commissioner Andrius Kubilius discusses efforts to boost the region's military capabilities, and calls for a rapid increase in spending by EU member nations. The bloc is trying to mobilize hundreds of billions of euros in defense funds and create a new security architecture to provide new support for Ukraine and protect against Russia. Kubilius talks with Oliver Crook on Bloomberg Television.

00:00 US policy toward Russia, Ukraine and Europe's response
01;17 Evaluating cost of boosting Europe's defense capabilities
03:13 Fiscal rules; building consensus; Hungary PM Viktor Orban
05:58 Italian proposal to extend NATO Article 5 protections to Ukraine
06:40 Starlink; reaction to Trump comments on US security guarantee
--------
More on Bloomberg Television and Markets

items.title: West Africa: Ghana President John Mahama on US Aid, Anti-LGBT Bill
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>Ghanaian President John Mahama says the US decision to freeze international aid will likely cause "blowback" for the world's largest economy. He also discusses the anti-LGBTQ bill, a proposed law that would make it illegal to be anything but heterosexual in Ghana. He speaks with Jennifer Zabasajja on Bloomberg Next Africa.

Watch the full episode of Bloomberg Next Africa: https://youtu.be/TTw0Elsvksc
--------

items.author: Bloomberg Television

items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content: <article>Blue Owl Capital Co-CEO Marc Lipschultz tells us what he would do with $10k and more at #BloombergInvest. Follow @Bloomberg_Live  for more https://trib.al/zKvk7wf</article>

items.title: Why the US Government Could Be at Risk Due to Lack of Top Tech Talent
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>At the hands of Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency, also known as DOGE, the US government is experiencing an alarming technology brain drain which risks leaving the country unprepared for any coming crisis, according to a Bloomberg Opinion article. Parmy Olson of Bloomberg Opinion has the details. Her views are her own.
--------
More on Bloomberg Television and Markets
